name: Major Depressive Disorder
summary: Major depressive disorder is a common condition with moderate heritability and strong environmental context in population studies. GENARCH provides educational summaries only.
adolescent_relevance: Often emerges in adolescence/young adulthood; GENARCH avoids individualized risk or clinical advice.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Stress exposure is associated with depression outcomes in cohorts; causal direction is complex.
Sleep disruption is associated with mental health outcomes; bidirectionality is common.
Associations vary and can be confounded by multiple factors.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Large MDD GWAS have been European-ancestry dominated historically.
population_equity.transferability_notes: PRS performance varies across ancestries due to LD and phenotype definitions.
population_equity.data_gaps: Exposure measurement heterogeneity and confounding are substantial.

references.title: Psychosocial stress and health outcomes: mechanisms and evidence (review)
Sleep duration and health outcomes: overview of population evidence (review)
WHO guidelines on physical activity and sedentary behaviour
